channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Declares Strait of Hormuz Closed Again as Ships Report Attacks
item.description: Iran reversed course on reopening the strategic waterway, warning it would continue to block transit as long as the U.S. blockade of ships from Iranian ports remained in effect.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: What to Know About the Bulgarian Election
item.description: The Black Sea country is holding its eighth election in five years, with Bulgarians yearning for the kind of prosperous life enjoyed by other Europeans.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.media:description: Former President Rumen Radev of Bulgaria speaking on Thursday during the last pre-election event of his coalition, Progressive Bulgaria. Most polls show the coalition leading, but not necessarily with enough support for a majority.

item.title: U.S. Officials Visited Havana to Lay Out Proposals for Cuban Reforms
item.description: The delegation told Cuba’s leadership that it had only a narrow window of time to make the economic and political changes demanded by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: A street in Havana last month. During the talks last week, a State Department official said, the American delegation insisted that President Trump was open to reaching a diplomatic solution to the crisis.

item.title: Kyiv Mass Shooting Kills 6 and Gunman Is Shot Dead After Taking Hostages in Grocery
item.description: It was the deadliest mass shooting in Ukraine in years, where firearms have proliferated since the war with Russia began.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut
item.media:description: Outside the supermarket in Kyiv, Ukraine, where a man took hostages and was killed by the police on Saturday.

item.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s ‘Mosquito Fleet’
item.description: Although much of the regular Iranian navy is destroyed, the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps can still deploy small, speedy boats to disrupt shipping.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps speedboat and navy ship in the Persian Gulf in 2024.

item.title: Pope Says News Outlets Misread Some of His Remarks as Criticism of Trump
item.description: Pope Leo responded directly on Monday to a presidential attack. But since then, he said, some of his statements during his Africa trip have been misconstrued.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: El papa León XIV en Yaundé, capital de Camerún, el sábado.

item.title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
item.description: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev and Mariana Martínez
item.media:description: Delcy Rodríguez, Venezuela’s former vice president, in January in Caracas, the capital.

item.title: Trump Spat Gives Spain Leader Pedro Sánchez a Political Lifeline
item.description: To leftists abroad, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain is a hero for standing up to President Trump. At home, Mr. Trump is seen as Mr. Sánchez’s political savior from thorny domestic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain addressing Parliament in March.

item.title: Carney’s Liberal Majority Reshapes Fortunes to the Left and the Right
item.description: Mark Carney’s brand of centrist politics and his invitation to floor crossers with conservative convictions into the Liberal Party’s ranks have reshaped the country’s political economy.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney with new members of the Liberal caucus, from left: Tatiana Auguste (Terrebonne), Danielle Martin (University-Rosedale) and Doly Begum (Scarborough Southwest) on Wednesday in Ottawa.

item.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Colonialism
item.description: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Cláudio C. Silva and John Eligon
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Luanda, Angola, on Saturday. Angola’s is the oldest Catholic community in southern Africa, dating back more than 500 years.

item.title: Trump Tries to Stop Drug Smuggling From South America. Drug Smugglers Invent New Tactics.
item.description: Authorities are using drones, troops and A.I. to crack down on cocaine smuggling. But gangs are finding new ways to move record amounts of drugs across the world.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova, Max Bearak and Federico Rios
item.category: US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

item.title: A Mild-Mannered Pope Finds His Voice
item.description: Leo XIV has had a reputation for being cautious. But since President Trump attacked him at the start of his Africa trip, he has been more combative.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday. His strident speeches in Africa this week have marked a shift in his rhetoric.

item.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
item.description: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear, Catherine Porter, Jane Bradley and Christopher F. Schuetze

item.title: Starmer’s Credibility Just Took Another Body Blow Over the Mandelson Scandal
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer appears to have been kept in the dark repeatedly over Peter Mandelson, the Jeffrey Epstein associate — fueling an image of weakness.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in Paris on Friday. “That I wasn’t told that Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting when he’s appointed is staggering,” he said.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Euan Ward, Leily Nikounazar and Somini Sengupta

item.title: Hezbollah is willing to cooperate with a cease-fire with Israel. For now.
item.description: The leader of the Iran-backed militia said that a more durable peace with Israel would require the fulfillment of a list of long-standing demands.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Protesters waved an Iranian flag and flags for Hezbollah in downtown Beirut during a demonstration to show support for Hezbollah last week.

item.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Declares Strict Control of Vital Shipping Route
item.description: The day after Iran declared the vital waterway open, it reversed course, injecting new peril into navigation there.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: Six Are Missing After Cargo Ship Is Found Overturned Near Guam
item.description: The ship, the Mariana, was found overturned days after the Coast Guard lost contact with its crew, after a super typhoon struck.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.media:credit: U.S. Coast Guard, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The U.S. Coast Guard is searching for six crew members from the Mariana, a U.S.-registered dry cargo vessel, which was found overturned in the Pacific.

item.title: More Displaced Lebanese Head Home as Cease-Fire Is Tested
item.description: The truce appeared to mostly hold even as Israel said it had carried out strikes on what it called “terrorists” approaching its forces in southern Lebanon. Separately, a U.N. peacekeeper was killed.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Sarah Chaayto and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Lebanese residents returned to their homes in the south on Saturday.

item.title: Trading Spaces
item.description: With spring comes the urge to spring-clean. It’s about so much more than just organizing your closet.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Who Is the New Leader of Hungary?
item.description: Our reporter Andrew Higgins describes why Hungary’s voters chose Peter Magyar over Viktor Orban in a landslide, ending Orban’s 16 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins, Christina Thornell and Leila Medina

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Euan Ward, Leily Nikounazar and Somini Sengupta

item.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
item.description: The Trump administration has loosened restrictions on Russian oil exports since the war in the Middle East began to rattle energy markets in March.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that the U.S. government would not renew the sanctions exemption on Russian oil already at sea.

item.title: New PEPFAR Data Show Worrying Declines in Testing and Treatment for H.I.V.
item.description: The numbers are the first to quantify the effect of the Trump administration’s shutdown and restarting of a program that has saved millions of lives worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
item.media:description: A woman held H.I.V. medication that she received through funding from the U.S. President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief, or PEPFAR, along with her hospital records book, at her home in Harare, Zimbabwe, last year.

item.title: Trump Frames Iran War as All but Over in Optimistic Social Media Flurry
item.description: Iranian officials did not confirm most of Mr. Trump’s claims and disputed several of them.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump during an event in Las Vegas on Thursday.

item.title: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open, but adds that it is still under Tehran’s supervision.
item.description: Ab
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attendees at a rally in support of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Amid Conflicting Messages From Trump and Iran, Hopes for Peace Deal Emerge
item.description: On a day when both Iran and the United States declared the Strait of Hormuz opened, hopes for an agreement rose. But statements from President Trump and Iranian leaders about negotiations were sometimes at odds.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Peter Eavis, Pranav Baskar and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: An Explosion Rattles a Toronto Neighborhood. A Drake Video Was to Blame.
item.description: A blast on Thursday night turned out to be for a video shoot, but it unnerved residents who had lived through a propane plant explosion in 2008.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Drake performing in London last year. He posted two images on social media of an explosion that startled residents near Downsview Park in Toronto.

item.title: Rescuers Try to Save Timmy, a Whale Stranded Off Germany
item.description: A month of efforts to help a stranded humpback escape the Baltic Sea have culminated in a tourist and media spectacle, with no guarantee of success.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.media:description: Off Poel Island in northern Germany on Thursday. A humpback whale has been stranded in the area for almost a month.

item.title: The U.S. blockade on ships will continue until Trump ends it, says Central Command chief.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Adm. Brad Cooper, the head of U.S. Central Command, during a news conference at the Pentagon on Thursday.

item.title: Carney Courts Investors to Reduce Canada’s Economic Dependence on U.S.
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has set up a meeting and a new office to speed project approvals and make Canada more attractive to investors.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada on Parliament Hill this week in Ottawa.

item.title: Mark Mobius, Pioneering Investor in Emerging Markets, Dies at 89
item.description: Gaining a reputation as the brilliant, risk-taking “Indiana Jones” of his field, he encouraged investors to take chances on Asia, Africa, Latin America and Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.category: Franklin Templeton Limited Duration Income Trust
item.media:credit: K.Y. Cheng/South China Morning Post, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mark Mobius in 2001. Working for Franklin Templeton in the late 1980s, he started one of the first investment funds anywhere dedicated to emerging markets.

item.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
item.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows the tunnel complex at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month. Last fall, satellite images revealed that Iran had advanced construction at the site soon after U.S. forces disabled the country’s three main nuclear facilities in June.

item.title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
item.description: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Yuri Gripas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Howard Lutnick, the U.S. Commerce Secretary, in Washington in February.

item.title: Europe Wants to Help Restore Shipping in the Strait. It Just Isn’t Sure How.
item.description: Amid conflicting reports about the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, European leaders gathered on Friday to coordinate a plan to guard it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, President Emmanuel Macron of France, Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the United Kingdom and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy arriving for a conference on navigation through the Strait of Hormuz at the Élysée Palace in Paris on Friday.

item.title: Why Lebanon’s Government Has Struggled to Contain Hezbollah
item.description: Lebanon’s government has been caught between Western demands to disarm the Shiite Muslim militant group and fears of inflaming sectarian tensions.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Hezbollah flag at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, last month.

item.title: U.K. Police Investigate Claims Drones Targeted Israeli Embassy in London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police said that the building had not been attacked but that they were assessing “discarded items” in Kensington Gardens, a nearby park, which was cordoned off.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Officers in Kensington Gardens in London on Friday.

item.title: New York New Jersey Stadium? Why MetLife Is Changing Its Name for the World Cup
item.description: This summer, FIFA is putting its stamp on the venue for the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: People in New York and New Jersey know it as MetLife Stadium, but that will not be the case for the coming World Cup.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Is Open During Cease-Fire in Lebanon, U.S. and Iran Say
item.description: Statements from President Trump and Iran aimed to raise confidence in the safety of the waterway, but shipping experts said risks remained.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo ships line up in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from the United Arab Emirates in March.

item.title: ‘I Just Want to Be Back’: Thousands Rush South in Lebanon Under Cease-Fire
item.description: Lebanese people who had been displaced by fighting expressed a mix of excitement and uncertainty about a pause in Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire: What to Know
item.description: The pause in fighting would remove a major hindrance to the U.S.-Iran peace talks, if it holds.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Families make their way home on Friday after the announcement of a cease-fire in Lebanon.

item.title: In northern Israel, relief at the cease-fire is overshadowed by unease.
item.description: Residents are wary that the truce may offer only a temporary reprieve after weeks of rocket fire by Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A street in the northern city of Kiryat Shmona, near Israel’s border with Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: Lebanon’s Cease-Fire Buys Time, Not a Way Out
item.description: The country’s leadership secured a truce through risky talks with Israel, but the government now finds itself caught between competing pressures.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A family returning to Dahiya, Lebanon, after the cease-fire on Friday.

item.title: Myanmar Frees Ousted President, but Not Daw Aung San Suu Kyi
item.description: U Win Myint is a close ally of Ms. Aung San Suu Kyi, the country’s civilian leader. Both were forced from power by the military in a 2021 coup and imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: Myanmar’s then-president, U Win Myint, at an event in Naypyidaw, the capital, in 2019. He was released from a prison in central Myanmar on Friday.

item.title: ‘I Would Rather Be a Dwarf’: A Comic in Botswana Prizes His Difference
item.description: Johnson Masase has found local fame, and self acceptance, through performances that play on people’s ignorance about his genetic condition and his 3-foot-4 inch frame.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Yvonne Mooka and Joao Silva

item.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Leaves Netanyahu in an Uncomfortable Spot
item.description: Most Israelis wanted the fight against Hezbollah to continue. But Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics say he is showing that he cannot, or will not, stand up to President Trump.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Thousands of families made their way along the highway south of Beirut and Saida, Lebanon, on Friday after the cease-fire with Israel was announced.

item.title: Starmer Fires Senior U.K. Civil Servant Over Mandelson Revelations
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office confirmed that Peter Mandelson, a Jeffrey Epstein associate, failed security checks before he became Britain’s envoy to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Olly Robbins in London in 2019. Mr. Robbins, the top civil servant in the British Foreign Office, was fired on Thursday night.

item.title: Iran War Forces America’s Friends in Asia to Court Its Rivals
item.description: The severe disruption of oil supplies from the Middle East as a result of the war has prompted South Korea and the Philippines to make deals with Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Javier C. Hernández, Choe Sang-Hun and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Ezra Acayan for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gas station in Manila. In the Philippines, America’s oldest treaty ally in Asia, the oil shortage prompted officials to declare a national emergency.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Euan Ward and Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Trump says he might go to Pakistan if an Iran deal is signed there.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters outside the White House on Thursday.

item.title: 10-Day Cease-Fire Between Israel and Lebanon Goes Into Effect
item.description: The pause in the fighting between Israeli forces and Iranian-allied Hezbollah militants may help smooth the way to a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Isabel Kershner, Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday. Hundreds of thousands of Lebanese have been forced by the fighting to leave their homes.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Greatest Mystery
item.description: The creator of Bitcoin has hidden behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto for 17 years.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: The cease-fire goes into effect after Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire.
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese officials had confirmed they would implement the truce. Hezbollah acknowledged the cease-fire, but did not directly address whether it would follow it.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Euan Ward, Tyler Pager and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man observing the aftermath of an attack in Corniche Mazraa from his home in Beirut on Monday.

item.title: Forced into a corner by the U.S., Netanyahu agrees to a cease-fire in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Mandelson, a Friend of Epstein’s, Became U.S. Ambassador Despite Failing Security Vetting
item.description: Britain’s foreign office overruled vetting officials in granting Peter Mandelson, a friend of Jeffrey Epstein, the highest level of security clearance, the government said.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson, then Britain’s ambassador to the United States, at the ambassador’s residence in Washington, in February last year.

item.title: Despite Cease-Fire, Iran’s Hackers Haven’t Logged Off
item.description: Tehran’s digital warriors have continued to seek ways to gain an advantage in the conflict in a new phase of cyberspace operations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Tehran this week. Iran is trying to keep up pressure on the United States and Israel with cyberattacks but also positioning itself to mount a bigger retaliation if peace talks do not resume.

item.title: The Long-Term Plan to Scrub Carbon From the Sky
item.description: Microsoft is pulling back from efforts to remove carbon from the atmosphere. But the nascent industry’s proponents say they are thinking in decades, not years.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.media:description: A carbon capture facility in Tracy, Calif., in 2023.

item.title: Bahrain Charges Intelligence Officer in the Death of a Detainee
item.description: The officer was charged with assault leading to death, in the case of a man who was accused by Bahraini authorities of espionage during a domestic crackdown tied to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising over Bahrain’s capital, Manama, after a reported Iranian drone strike last month.
